reviews.review_title: A great trip down memory lane
reviews.review_description:
A truly nostalgic experience for those who want to relieve the feelings of holding those game boxes in their virtual hands. Oldies but goldies, games that will never die. They will forever live in this virtual museum. 
Love it! Can’t wait to customize my room in the future and decorate it with my collection of games.

Just had to buy the Honoured Donator Add-On for all the hard work that has gone into this free app. 🥰

This is great, really brings back some memories.  Great work, thanks!

Just a suggestion, i notice i can't use the app without doing a Meta space setup.  Perhaps the app could just use the Meta passthrough and normal boundary (stationary or roomscale)?

Thanks again!

Very nice app! Pure nostalgia. And for early access, it's completely sufficient. The only thing I miss is a filter function by language, so that you only see languages you can actually read. Additionally, a simpler background room for VR would be nice. Currently, I only have a Quest 2 with decent passthrough, which unfortunately dampens the immersion a bit. So far, 4 out of 5 stars. Keep up the good work! :)

developer_response.body: Thank you very much, and for the suggestions!

reviews.review_description: Great idea, great implementation. Thank you for this.

My thoughts: https://youtu.be/Cw0KEGj-wkM
Great app for collectors, avid gamers, those want to learn more about gaming history and discover more detailed information about specific classic retro games. Think about visiting something like Super Potato (the Japanese retro gaming store) but in your home!

This is janky, it is a work in progress.

Give it 10minutes! You will have worked out how everything functions, and a wonderful experience reveals itself!

So much nostalgia and so much information!

To behold the breathtaking artwork of box art in high definition is something that was missing in VR.

Bring on multiplayer, I am sure many people will use this!

Thank you so much!

reviews.review_title: Absolutely amazing trip to the past
reviews.review_description:
I used to absolutely love reading the text and admiring the art on my old big box PC games when I was little, so this is a nostalgia dream come true! If only the smell of the manuals could be recreated too! A video of my app test is at the link below (remove the space before 'be'):

https://youtu. be/GjUxZgcEDHM?si=Ng3HyM8gqlrHQy18

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your review, I really appreciate it!!

reviews.review_description: I wouldn't have thought I would feel moved when holding the box of Thief 2 in my hands again, several decades after my brothers and I saved up and went to the game store in high anticipation, but here I am! Game Box Museum is a rush of nostalgia, with high quality textures of hundreds of games. All it needs is a bit of usability polish. Highly recommend taking a look!

Beautiful and many thanks for this great app! Now I would like to add a few Amiga games, unfortunately this function does not work properly with the mobile app, but I have already written this via Discord.

Sorting by system would be good, and there are still bugs in the software and the translation.

I wish you much success for the further development!

reviews.review_title: Fun way to enjoy videogame artwork
reviews.review_description: Cool app!!!  Like window shopping in a 90's-early 2000s boutique game store.  So many games I've never heard of.  Boxes look cool when you blow up their size to see more details.  Thanks for putting this together!

Idea = 5 Stars
implementation = 1 Star

so buggy & laggy and mixed reality only.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your kindness with putting the rating at 5 stars, I appreciate it. I'm actively rushing to get some fixes in.

reviews.review_description:
Just appears to highlight a box but no information option is showing. 
idea is good though but U.I.needs work.
*Reply, ‘Print’ option should say ‘Info’ which is one of the reasons that the U.I. needs attention.
I’ll wait for the update and then try again.

developer_response.body:
After selecting a box in the overview, press print and the box will appear under the menu. 
You can see what interaction options you have with boxes as tooltips on the controller. Trigger opens a radial menu which you control with the thumbstick. If you select "Show info" it will summon the tablet.
Let me know if you have any questions! I am uploading some bugfixes right now.
